🏷 HESTER BIOSCIENCES: Exits Texas Lifesciences Investment

• 🤝 Hester Biosciences has sold its remaining 11% stake in Texas Lifesciences Pvt. Ltd.

• 📦 Transferred 5,43,248 equity shares to the existing shareholders of Texas Lifesciences.

• 💰 The transaction was completed for a total consideration of ₹2.3 crore.

• ✅ Following the sale, the company's shareholding in Texas Lifesciences has reduced to Nil, marking a complete exit from the investment.

💧 CONCORD ENVIRO SYSTEMS: Invests ₹10.54 Crore in Wholly-Owned Subsidiary

• 💰 Invested ₹10.54 crore in wholly-owned subsidiary Rochem Separation Systems (India) Pvt. Ltd. (RSSIPL) through a rights issue.

• 🏗 The investment will fund the brownfield expansion project at the subsidiary's Vasai facility.

• 📋 The investment is in line with the objects of the company's IPO.

• 💧 RSSIPL is engaged in the manufacturing of water and wastewater treatment systems and related environmental solutions.

• ✅ RSSIPL continues to remain a 100% wholly-owned subsidiary of Concord Enviro Systems.

⚡️ HAVELLS INDIA: Enters Battery Energy Storage (BESS) Segment

• 🤝 Entered into a strategic collaboration with Norway-based Pixii AS to develop Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) for the Indian market.

• 🏭 The partnership will cater to residential, commercial and industrial (C&I) segments.

• 🔋 Havells will leverage its manufacturing capabilities and distribution network, while Pixii will provide its energy storage technology.

• 📈 The collaboration will follow a phased approach, including pilot projects, co-development of storage solutions and eventual local manufacturing in India.

• 🌱 The partnership aims to support India's clean energy transition and tap into the fast-growing BESS market.

☀️ VIKRAM SOLAR: Signs 130 MW Solar Cell Supply Agreement

• 🤝 Entered into a Solar Cell Supply Agreement with Evervolt Solar Technology India Pvt. Ltd.

• 🔋 The agreement is for the procurement of 130 MW of Mono-PERC 10BB DCR-compliant solar cells.

• 📅 Supplies will be made from July 2026 to March 2027.

• 🏭 The procurement will support the company's domestic solar module manufacturing.

• ✅ The agreement is not a related-party transaction and does not grant any special rights.

🌱 HCLTECH: HCLFoundation Partners with UP Government

• 🤝 HCLFoundation has signed an MoU with the Uttar Pradesh Urban Development Department.

• 🏙 The partnership will improve waste management in Agra, Prayagraj and Hardoi under the Swachh Bharat Mission.

• 💻 The collaboration will focus on IT-enabled solutions, pilot projects, strategic planning and policy reforms.

• 🚛 In Hardoi, 10 e-drain carts, 10 e-garbage loaders and 3 reverse vending machines have been launched.

• 🌳 HCLFoundation will also develop 3 model markets, 3 model wards and undertake city beautification initiatives in Hardoi.

• ❌ Company says it has not received any award letter, contract or official communication confirming the tender award

• 📝 States the news report is speculative and no material event has crystallised as of now

• ⚠️ Torrent Pharma has initiated a voluntary recall of select batches of Semalix Injection 2 mg & 4 mg disposable pens

TCS Q1FY27 Results:-

➤ Revenue ₹72,275 Cr (+13.9% YoY & +2.2% QoQ)

➤ Total Contract Value (TCV) US$9.5 Billion

➤ Annualized AI Revenue US$2.6 Billion (+13.6% QoQ)

➤ Workforce 593,798

➤ LTM Attrition (IT Services) 13.6%

➤ Dividend ₹12/share (Record Date: 15 Jul 2026, Payment Date: 31 Jul 2026)

➤ Won an US$800 million AI-led mega deal with SKF

➤ Signed a multi-million-dollar strategic partnership with ServiceNow

➤ Secured a multi-million-dollar AI transformation deal with a Europe-based Fortune Global 50 company

➤ Continued strong momentum across AI, software engineering, modernization, cybersecurity, sovereign cloud and platform transformation

➤ Expanded ecosystem partnerships with Anthropic and Mistral AI to strengthen AI offerings

➤ Management Commentary
✓ Q1 FY27 reflected continued growth despite macro and geopolitical challenges

✓ Strong US$9.5 billion TCV, including the marquee US$800 million SKF AI transformation deal

✓ Annualized AI revenue reached US$2.6 billion

✓ Growing client investments in AI, modernization, cybersecurity, sovereign cloud and platform simplification, along with better deal conversion and client mining, position TCS for sustained growth

✓ Strong growth across multiple service lines during the quarter

✓ Won several AI-led transformation deals focused on AI optimization and innovation

✓ Expanded capabilities in AI-powered IT operations, software engineering, modernization, AI-first process redesign, SaaS implementation and Autonomous GBS

✓ Strengthened the AI ecosystem through strategic partnerships with Anthropic and Mistral

✓ Implemented annual wage hikes in Q1

✓ Continued investments in partnerships and AI capabilities to enhance long-term competitiveness

✓ Maintained disciplined execution alongside industry-leading profitability and return ratios

✓ Completed annual salary increments globally and aligned compensation with the new India Labour Code

✓ Continued investments in AI infrastructure and next-generation skill development to build a future-ready workforce while fostering an inclusive and trusted workplace

reports.text:
Real Estate Pre-sales growth over last few years

Top 4 developers combined Pre-sales increased from ₹213 Bn in FY21 to ₹1,049 Bn in FY26 (5x growth)

Branded developers are capturing a larger share of the market

Most real estate companies follow the project completion method, which basically means revenue can be recognized only when the project is delivered

So the current P&L will not fully reflect the current business position

As projects start getting completed, Pre-Sales will start flowing into the P&L

A lot of pre-sales have already happened over the last 5 years, but the revenue recognition will come as projects get delivered

Unrecognized revenue for many developers is sitting at record levels

The interesting part is that many of these projects are in the luxury / premium category, where embedded margins are also higher

As deliveries pick up over the next few years, P&L of real estate companies will start looking robust

This delivery cycle can also benefit many building material players, as project execution & handovers increase

• 📊 The bond market is signaling that even cash-rich tech giants face a higher cost of capital as AI spending accelerates

Sathlokhar Synergys E&C Global Limited Secures New Orders Worth ₹75.52 Cr (excluding GST); Revised FY27 Confirmed Order Book Reaches ₹915.74 Cr (excluding GST)

The Company has secured five new orders across MEP, civil and electrical works, strengthening its execution pipeline across footwear manufacturing, industrial facilities, consumer products and food processing sectors.

Key Highlights:

* Additional confirmed orders worth approximately ₹75.52 Cr (excluding GST)
* Revised FY27 confirmed order book at approximately ₹915.74 Cr (excluding GST), up from ₹840.22 Cr (excluding GST)
* New orders to be executed over the next 3 to 9 months
* Orders span MEP, civil and electrical works across multiple sectors

Order Details:

1. High Glory Footwear India Private Limited

* MEP works for A5 and A6 at the proposed factory in Kallakurichi, Tamil Nadu
* Order Value: ₹40.59 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before December 2026
* Subsidiary of Taiwan based Pou Chen Corporation Industrial Group, the world’s largest branded athletic and casual footwear manufacturer

2. Grand Atlantia Panapakkam SEZ Developers Private Limited

* MEP works for the proposed building at SIPCOT Park, Panapakkam, Ranipet
* Order Value: ₹25.55 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before October 2026
* Part of Taiwan based Hong Fu Industrial Group, which is investing approximately ₹1,500 Cr in a mega non leather footwear manufacturing facility in Ranipet

3. Anabond Limited

* Civil works at APIC Industrial Park, Andhra Pradesh
* Order Value: ₹19.48 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before January 2027

4. Reliance Consumer Products Limited (Subsidiary of Reliance Industries Limited and producer of CAMPA COLA beverages)

* New project site establishment civil works in Bijapur, Karnataka
* Order Value: ₹1.22 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before October 2026

5. Karaikal Iyangars Foods Limited (Known as Iyengar Bakery)

* Electrical works in Karaikal, Pondicherry
* Order Value: ₹2.26 Cr, including GST
* Execution Timeline: Before September 2026

The new orders further strengthen the Company’s position as an integrated EPC execution partner while expanding its capabilities across MEP, civil and electrical works.

• 📈 PARA has also approved conversion of an outstanding loan of ₹27.04 crore into 1,56,301 equity shares at ₹1,730 per share (₹10 face value + ₹1,720 premium)

🟢 Impact: Positive (The restructuring simplifies the group structure, gives SAR Televenture direct ownership of its telecom infrastructure business, and enhances operational control.)

**NOMURA: Cuts Q1FY27 Estimates for OMCs**

• Nomura expects **HPCL, BPCL and IOCL** to report **Q1FY27 EBITDA losses** of around **₹13,900 crore**, **₹15,800 crore** and **₹17,300 crore**, respectively.

• The losses are driven by **higher crude oil prices**, as OMCs were unable to fully pass on increased fuel costs to consumers.

• **Retail marketing margins** are estimated to decline to a **loss of ₹20–21/litre** in Q1FY27, compared with a **loss of around ₹5/litre** in Q4FY26.

• **Higher Gross Refining Margins (GRMs)** are expected to partially offset the marketing losses.

• **Higher refinery transfer prices** and **rupee depreciation** also impacted profitability.

reports.text: GRAVITA INDIA GROWTH CATALYST

INDIA | Battery Storage Demand Outpaces Cell Manufacturing

Market Snapshot
• BESS tender pipeline reaches 260 GWh.
• Domestic cell capacity only 2 GWh.
• Supply-demand gap widens sharply.
• Storage demand accelerates rapidly.

Industry Growth
• Installed BESS reaches 8.7 GWh.
• Capacity rises 11x in six months.
• Expected to exceed 10 GWh.
• Storage adoption gains momentum.

Tender Activity
• H1 FY26 tenders total 47 GWh.
• Pipeline expands to 260 GWh.
• Grid-scale deployment accelerating.
• Merchant projects drive additions.

Long-Term Outlook
• Storage need at 888 GWh.
• Target year 2035–36.
• Manufacturing expansion remains crucial.
• Import dependence likely near term.

Key Takeaway
India's battery storage market is witnessing rapid demand growth, but domestic cell manufacturing remains far behind. Bridging this capacity gap will be critical to achieving the country's long-term renewable energy and energy storage ambitions.

GLP-1 DRUG MARKET | June Sales Moderate After Record High

Market Performance
• June sales decline to ₹227 crore.
• May sales stood at ₹236 crore.
• Market remains nearly 3x YoY.
• MAT reaches ₹2,055 crore.
• MAT grows 238% YoY.

Category Trends
• Tirzepatide sales ease in June.
• Semaglutide sales remain resilient.
• Generic launches drove earlier surge.
• Growth now begins normalizing.
• Market expansion starts plateauing.

Key Observations
• Generics account for 82% volumes.
• Innovator brands continue gaining traction.
• Physician confidence remains strong.
• Patient adoption continues expanding.

Industry Outlook
• FY26 pharma growth forecast upgraded.
• Market growth seen at 11.3%.
• Anti-diabetic therapies remain strong.
• Healthcare demand remains healthy.

Key Takeaway
June's decline appears to reflect a post-patent normalization rather than weakening demand. The GLP-1 market remains structurally strong, supported by rising patient adoption, resilient innovator brands and continued growth in India's pharmaceutical market.

• 🔍 Company is investigating the root cause and taking corrective measures to ensure product quality

• 🚚 Commercial supplies will be delayed temporarily until the issue is resolved

• 🩺 No impact on patient safety, based on existing global regulatory filings

• 🌍 Dr. Reddy's said it remains committed to ensuring reliable global supply of the metabolic therapy

• 📞 Management will host a conference call today to discuss the issue and answer investor queries

RAVINDRA ENERGY: ASSOCIATE EIM SIGNS 500 MWh BATTERY PACK DEAL WITH CATL 🔋⚡

• 🤝 Associate company Energy In Motion (EIM) signed an agreement with CATL for supply of 500 MWh LFP battery cells and battery packs in India

• 🚛 Batteries will power EIM's Ashwa 55-ton E-Tractor 4x2 heavy commercial electric vehicles with 400.6 kWh battery systems

• 🔄 EIM becomes the launch customer in India for CATL's new-generation battery systems, customized for battery-swapping applications

Tata Steel Limited Q1FY27 Business Update

➤ Production
✓ India 5.82 MnT (+11% YoY, -6% QoQ)
✓ Netherlands 1.55 MnT (-9% YoY, -5% QoQ)
✓ Thailand 0.33 MnT (Flat YoY, +3% QoQ)

➤ Deliveries
✓ India 5.17 MnT (+9% YoY, -16% QoQ)
✓ Netherlands 1.40 MnT (-7% YoY, -18% QoQ)
✓ UK 0.48 MnT (-20% YoY, -8% QoQ)
✓ Thailand 0.33 MnT (-3% YoY, -3% QoQ)

➤ India Business Highlights
✓ Automotive & Special Products: Best-ever Q1 volumes of ~0.9 MnT, with Kalinganagar ramp-up driving ~20% YoY growth in high-end products.

✓ Branded Products & Retail: Best-ever Q1 volumes of ~1.7 MnT
    ✓ Tata Tiscon: Best-ever Q1 volumes, +33% YoY
    ✓ Tata Steelium: +41% YoY growth

✓ Industrial Products & Projects: Volumes exceeded ~1.6 MnT, supported by growth in shipbuilding, containers and data centres

✓ Downstream Businesses: Tubes, Tinplate, Wires and Colours recorded growth, with Tubes and Tinplate achieving best-ever Q1 volumes

✓ E-commerce GMV (Aashiyana & DigECA): ₹2,200 Cr (+61% YoY)

➤ International Operations
✓ Netherlands: Operations impacted by the Direct Sheet Plant shutdown in Apr'26; trial runs underway ahead of full restart

✓ UK: Continued customer servicing through downstream processing while work progresses on the ~3 MnTPA Electric Arc Furnace at Port Talbot

✓ Thailand: Production and deliveries remained stable at 0.33 MnT

KP ENERGY: COMMISSIONING OF 50.4 MW WIND POWER PROJECT

KP Energy has successfully commissioned a 50.4 MW Wind Power Project located in Vanki, Nakhatrana, Kutch, Gujarat. 

The project consists of 16 Suzlon Wind Turbine Generators (WTGs), each with an individual capacity of 3.15 MW. 

The Commercial Operation Date (COD) for the project was achieved on July 8, 2026. 

This wind project was developed for NTPC Renewable Energy Limited. 

The commissioning reinforces KP Energy's execution track record in wind Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) and Operations & Maintenance (O&M), while supporting India's renewable energy transition.

Kusumgar Limited – Our Take on Post-Listing Prospects

Not a GMP take. A "Will it Hold Up Post-Listing?" take

📌 The Setup

- IPO Size: ₹650 crore
- Price Band: ₹398–419
- Opens: 8 July
- Closes: 10 July
- Listing: 15 July
- Anchor Book: ₹193.95 crore from 23 institutions at ₹419
- Current GMP: ~40%

- That's the number everyone's chasing—not the one that matters most.

🚩 100% Offer for Sale (OFS)

- Entire ₹650 crore issue is an Offer for Sale.
- Zero proceeds go to the company.
- No funding for capex.
- No debt reduction.
- Pure promoter stake monetisation.

📉 Revenue & PAT Declined in FY26

- Revenue fell from ₹779 crore (FY25) to ₹692 crore (FY26) (-11.2%).
- PAT declined from ₹112 crore (FY25) to ₹98.2 crore (FY26) (-12.3%).
- FY25 benefited from a one-time ₹222 crore parachute order.
- That was a peak year, not a sustainable trend.

🏭 Capacity Utilisation Fell to 62%

- Manufacturing capacity nearly tripled between FY24 and FY25 after the Karanj facility.
- Capacity utilisation:
  - 79.7% (FY24)
  - 84.4% (FY25)
  - 62.5% (FY26)
- Additional capex continued despite lower utilisation.
- Capacity has been built ahead of demand.

🎯 Tender-Driven & Concentrated Business

- Aerospace & Defence contributed 54.6% of FY26 revenue.
- Direct Government revenue declined from 34.65% (FY25) to 15.09% (FY26).
- Largest customer contributed 11.1% of revenue.
- Top 10 customers accounted for 59.5% of revenue.
- Business is largely based on purchase orders, not long-term contracts.

💰 Premium Valuation Despite Degrowth

- Valuation stands at around 45x P/E.
- Peer average is around 40x (Garware, Arvind, SRF).
- Peers delivered growth.
- Kusumgar reported declining earnings.
- Premium valuation leaves limited room for re-rating.

📋 CEO ESOP Increased Sharply Before IPO

- ESOP expense increased:
  - ₹0 (FY24)
  - ₹3.5 crore (FY25)
  - ₹19.9 crore (FY26)
- Majority allocated to CEO Ankur Kothari.
- ESOP scheme was introduced during the IPO preparation period.

🧾 Consultancy Fee to Former Promoter Director

- Yogesh Kusumgar resigned as Director in March 2025.
- Received ₹0.6 crore as consultancy fees during FY26.
- Nature and scope of consultancy are not clearly detailed in the RHP.

📊 Working Capital Deteriorated

- Receivable days increased:
  - 33 → 26 → 123 (FY24–FY26)
- Inventory days increased:
  - 112 → 64 → 103 (FY24–FY26)
- Both expanded significantly while revenue declined.

🏢 Related Promoter Entities

- Only one Group Company disclosed: Specialty Fabrics Pvt Ltd.
- CMD is also associated with:
  - Kusumgar Aerospace
  - Four S Weaving
  - Concord Weaving Preparatory
  - Toray Kusumgar Advanced Textile
  - V.B. Kusumgar & Co
- The June 2025 non-compete agreement covers only two of these entities.

🧭 Our Take

Listing Perspective

- Strong anchor participation could support listing performance.
- Positive GMP suggests healthy initial demand.

Long-Term Perspective

- FY25 appears to have been driven by a one-off order.
- FY26 already reflects a slowdown.
- Capacity utilisation has weakened.
- Valuation assumes growth that has yet to reappear.
- 100% OFS with rising working capital is not an ideal combination.

Bottom Line

Watch Q1 FY27 and Q2 FY27 performance before concluding whether this is a genuine long-term compounder or primarily a listing-gains opportunity.

The company successfully deployed its first premium connection casing pipes at an Oil India Ltd. gas well in Assam, marking its entry into the premium casing & tubing market.

UDTL supplied and installed 5,000 metres of 7-inch premium production casing, validating its capabilities in the high-value oilfield segment.

The company estimates India's premium casing & tubing market at around ₹2,600 crore annually, with demand of nearly 1.2 lakh tonnes.

The brokerage expects the acquisition to deliver around **4% EPS accretion** to FY28 proforma estimates.

**Key Takeaway**
CLSA remains bullish on Persistent, expecting the Nagarro acquisition to strengthen growth, profitability, and earnings.

**JEFFERIES | INDIA STRATEGY – Q1 FY27 Preview**

Revenue growth is expected to reach a **13-quarter high**, supported by resilient economic activity and rising **Nominal GDP**.

Higher **energy and input costs** may pressure margins for select domestic companies.

Earnings excluding **Oil & Gas** and **Metals** are expected to grow around **12% YoY**.

**Lending Financials, Non-lending Financials** and **Telecom** are expected to deliver **15%+ YoY** earnings growth.

**Cement, Pharma, IndiGo** and **Chemicals** are likely to report a YoY decline in earnings.

**Key Takeaway**
Jefferies expects strong revenue momentum in Q1, with financials and telecom leading earnings growth despite margin pressures.

**GOLDMAN SACHS | L&T**

Maintained **Buy** rating with a **target price of ₹4,500**.

The **Middle East** has become the key driver of L&T's international business, contributing **40% of the FY26 order book** and **36% of FY26 order inflows**.

The company is well-positioned to benefit from investments in **renewables, clean hydrogen**, and resilience-led infrastructure spending.

Multi-year project visibility is expected to reduce earnings cyclicality and support long-term growth.

**Key Takeaway**
Goldman Sachs remains positive on L&T, citing strong Middle East exposure and long-term infrastructure opportunities.
Elara Capital maintains its 'Buy' rating on InterGlobe Aviation, expecting stronger Q1 earnings driven by robust airfare growth despite higher fuel costs

**MANKIND PHARMA | InCred View**

**Key Highlights**
- ADD rating initiated
- Target Price at ₹2,812
- Strategic business transformation
- Focus on R&D and niche therapies
- BSV acquisition strengthens portfolio
- Long-term growth opportunity

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **InCred** initiated coverage with an **ADD** rating and a **Target Price of ₹2,812**.
- **Business Transformation:** The brokerage believes Mankind Pharma is **transitioning from a volume- and distribution-led generics model** towards an **R&D-driven, niche therapies-focused business**, reflecting the maturity of its legacy growth model.
- **Strategic Acquisition:** The **BSV acquisition** provides a strong entry into the **specialty biologics** segment, enhancing the company's presence in high-value therapies.
- **Growth Strategy:** InCred noted that **recent growth has been driven more by portfolio acquisitions than organic volume expansion**, with the benefits of the transformation expected to materialize gradually over time.

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred is **constructive on Mankind Pharma's long-term prospects**, believing its **strategic shift toward specialty therapies, R&D and biologics** positions the company for sustainable growth, although the full benefits are expected to unfold gradually.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

- Valuation remains expensive

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Sell** with a **Target Price of ₹980**.
- **Volume Growth:** Estimated **Q1 FY27 volume growth** of **11% YoY**, indicating potential downside risk to JSPL's FY27 sales volume target of **10.5–11 million tonnes**, which implies **21–26% YoY growth**.
- **Profitability:** A **sharp correction in long steel prices** (down **over 15% since April 2026**) is likely to result in **EBITDA per tonne peaking in Q1 FY27**.
- **Earnings Outlook:** The brokerage expects **consensus EBITDA estimates to be revised downward** as steel realizations soften.
- **Valuation:** Based on **spot EBITDA per tonne** and expanded production volumes, **JSPL is trading at around 12x EV/EBITDA**, which the brokerage considers demanding.

**Key Takeaway**
- The brokerage remains **cautious on JSPL**, citing **slowing profitability, downside risk to volume guidance and expensive valuations**, despite continued capacity expansion.

reports.text: Technical Dashboard - 06.07.2026 (Front Wave Research)

**TRENT | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹2,733
- Revenue missed estimates
- Revenue productivity remained weak
- Store additions ahead of estimates
- Competitive pressures persist

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Sell** rating with a **Target Price of ₹2,733**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, below Citi's **23% estimate**.
- **Revenue Productivity:** **Average revenue per square foot** (assuming similar new-store size as TTM) **declined 12.2% YoY**, indicating continued weakness despite a favourable base.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store** and **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter, ahead of Citi's expectations of **0** and **6** stores, respectively.
- **Seasonality:** Citi noted that **Q1 is typically the weakest quarter for store additions**, making the expansion pace less of a concern.
- **Outlook:** The brokerage remains **cautious** due to **weak revenue per square foot, rising competition, store cannibalization and continued expansion into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi maintains a **negative stance on Trent**, believing that **declining store productivity, intensifying competition and expansion-related challenges** could weigh on future growth despite continued store additions.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TRENT | Bernstein View**

**Key Highlights**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹3,500
- Revenue slightly missed estimates
- Westside additions remained weak
- Zudio expansion stayed healthy
- FY27 outlook unchanged

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Bernstein** maintained its **Outperform (O-P)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹3,500**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, slightly below Bernstein's **20.5% estimate**.
- **Growth:** Revenue growth was **below expectations**, reflecting a softer start to the quarter.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store**, while **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter.
- **Store Additions:** Overall additions were **below Bernstein's expectations** and **lower than Q1 FY25**.
- **Outlook:** Bernstein noted that **Q1 is typically the slowest quarter for store additions** and therefore remains **not concerned about Trent achieving its accelerated FY27 store expansion guidance**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Bernstein continues to **favour Trent**, believing the **slight revenue miss and slower store additions are temporary**, while maintaining confidence in the company's **FY27 expansion plans and long-term growth trajectory**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TRENT | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹3,151
- Revenue broadly in line
- Store additions moderated
- Margin improvement expected
- Near-term stock weakness possible

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Overweight (OW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹3,151**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue (excluding GST)** grew **19% YoY**, slightly below Morgan Stanley's **21% estimate**, and broadly in line with **20% growth in Q4 FY26**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **19 net Zudio stores** and **1 Westside store** during the quarter, reflecting a **slower expansion pace** compared to the strong additions in Q4 FY26.
- **Margins:** Morgan Stanley expects **Q1 FY27 standalone EBITDA margin** to improve **100 bps YoY** to **18.5%**, compared with **18.6% in Q4 FY26**.
- **Stock View:** With the stock having **rallied around 20% over the past month**, Morgan Stanley believes the **slightly weaker-than-expected revenue print could lead to near-term profit booking**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **constructive on Trent's long-term outlook**, supported by **healthy revenue growth and margin expansion**, but expects **near-term stock performance to remain subdued** following the recent rally and slightly softer Q1 business update.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS | CLSA View**

**Key Highlights**
- Outperform rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹520
- Revenue beat estimates
- Domino's LFL exceeded expectations
- Strong standalone performance
- Eurasia business remained weak

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **CLSA** maintained its **Outperform (O-P)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹520**.
- **Standalone Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone sales** grew **9.2% YoY** to **₹1,849 Cr**, outperforming CLSA's **5% growth estimate** and broadly in line with consensus expectations.
- **Domino's India:** Reported **2.5% Like-for-Like (LFL) sales growth**, slightly ahead of CLSA's expectations.
- **Consolidated Revenue:** Increased **14.1% YoY**, reflecting continued growth across the group.
- **Eurasia Business:** Reported **-1.3% LFL growth**, indicating continued weakness in the international business.

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA remains **positive on Jubilant FoodWorks**, supported by **better-than-expected India business performance, improving same-store sales at Domino's India and healthy revenue growth**, although the **Eurasia business remains a key area to watch**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Equal-Weight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹486
- India business met expectations
- LFL growth improved sequentially
- Store expansion continued
- Margins remain key monitorable

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Equal-Weight (EW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹486**.
- **India Business:** Standalone **Q1 FY27 revenue** grew **9.2% YoY**, broadly in line with Morgan Stanley's **9% estimate**.
- **Same-Store Sales:** **Like-for-Like (LFL) growth** improved to **2.5%**, compared with **0.2% in Q4 FY26**, but was slightly below the brokerage's **3% estimate**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **58 new stores** during the quarter, ahead of Morgan Stanley's expectation of **50 stores**, resulting in a **12% YoY increase in store count**.
- **Consolidated Revenue:** Increased **14.1% YoY**, below Morgan Stanley's **18% growth estimate**.
- **Outlook:** Morgan Stanley noted that the **sequential improvement in India growth** is in line with management guidance, but **LFL growth remains below the company's long-term target of 5–7%**.
- **Margins:** The brokerage expects **standalone EBITDA margin** to moderate to **20%** in Q1 FY27 from **20.5% in Q4 FY26**, citing **inflationary pressures** as the key monitorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **neutral on Jubilant FoodWorks**, acknowledging the **improving sales trajectory and healthy store expansion**, while highlighting that **margin pressure and the pace of same-store sales recovery** will be the key factors to watch in the upcoming results.

- **Jewellery Performance:** **Domestic jewellery (excluding bullion)** reported **39% YoY growth**, ahead of **Citi's estimate of 35%**, despite **gold prices rising around 59% YoY**.
- **Growth Drivers:** Management attributed the strong performance to **healthy festive demand**, including **Akshaya Tritiya**, during the quarter.
- **Consumer Trends:** **Buyer growth** remained in the **early double digits**, while the **average ticket size** increased by **high double digits**.
- **Product Mix:** Both **plain gold** and **studded jewellery** businesses grew in the **mid-30% range**, indicating a **stable studded jewellery mix** (excluding gold coins).
- **Retail Expansion:** **Tanishq, Mia, Zoya and beYon** added **22 new stores** across India during the quarter.
- **Other Businesses:** **CaratLane** delivered **42% YoY growth**, while **Watches & Wearables (+23%)**, **EyeCare (+23%)** and **Emerging Businesses (+19%)** also reported strong growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi remains **positive on Titan**, supported by **better-than-expected jewellery growth, healthy consumer demand, continued retail expansion and strong performance across all major business segments**, reinforcing confidence in its long-term growth outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- HSBC remains **constructive on Titan**, supported by **better-than-expected jewellery growth, improving consumer demand, easing regulatory uncertainties and potential earnings upgrades**, reinforcing its positive long-term outlook.

**PG ELECTROPLAST | White Goods ODM Opportunity**

**Key Highlights**
- Leading ODM/OEM manufacturer
- Strong backward integration
- Compressor plant to boost margins
- Healthy earnings growth outlook

**Business Updates**
- PG Electroplast has evolved from a **plastic moulding company** into a leading **ODM/OEM manufacturer** for **room air conditioners, washing machines, TVs and consumer durables**, serving **70+ Indian and global brands**.
- Operates **12 manufacturing plants** and is currently the **second-largest ODM player** in **room air conditioners and washing machines**.
- Backward integration, including in-house manufacturing of key RAC components and an upcoming **compressor facility**, is expected to improve margins and cost competitiveness.
- Product business contribution increased to **~76% in FY26**, compared with **23% in FY20**, driven by rapid growth in the RAC segment.
- Despite a weak summer season, the company reported **FY26 revenue of ₹5,288 crore**, supported by strong execution and customer diversification.
- Revenue is estimated to grow at **~26% CAGR** during **FY26–FY28E**, while **PAT is projected to grow at ~56% CAGR**.

**Key Takeaway**
- PG Electroplast is **moving up the value chain** through backward integration and capacity expansion, positioning itself to benefit from the **outsourcing trend in India's white goods manufacturing**.

**BANKING SECTOR | Q1FY27 Business Update (Motilal Oswal)**

**Key Highlights**
- Credit growth accelerates to 16.3%
- Deposit growth improves to 12.3%
- Regional banks lead performance
- LDR strengthens across the sector

**Sector Snapshot**
- **26 of 33** listed universal banks have reported Q1FY27 business updates.
- Aggregate **credit growth** accelerated to **16.3% YoY** (vs. 13.9% in Q4FY26).
- Aggregate **deposit growth** improved to **12.3% YoY** (vs. 11.3% in Q4FY26).
- **Loan-to-Deposit Ratio (LDR)** increased by **114 bps QoQ** to **86.1%**.
- **CASA balances** remained stable at **11.0% YoY**.

**Cohort-wise Performance**

**Large Private Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.2% YoY**.
- Deposit growth strengthened to **15.3% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **97 bps QoQ**.
- Growth led by **Axis Bank**, while **HDFC Bank** showed recovery.

**New Age Private Banks**
- Credit growth accelerated to **13.5% YoY**.
- Deposit growth improved to **11.7% YoY**.
- LDR increased by **57 bps QoQ**.
- Growth supported by **AU Small Finance Bank, IDFC First Bank, RBL Bank** and **Yes Bank**.

**Public Sector Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.7% YoY**.
- Deposit growth rose to **10.7% YoY**.
- LDR expanded by **105 bps QoQ**.
- Strong momentum across major PSU banks.

**Regional Banks**
- Strongest-performing cohort with **19.3% YoY credit growth**.
- Deposit growth stood at **13.7% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **111 bps QoQ**.
- Healthy growth reported by **Karur Vysya Bank, South Indian Bank, CSB Bank, Tamilnad Mercantile Bank** and **Dhanlaxmi Bank**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Motilal Oswal expects **Q1FY27 to reflect broad-based strength across the banking sector**, with **faster credit growth, improving deposit mobilisation and stable CASA trends**. **Regional banks continue to outperform**, while **large private banks are witnessing improving business momentum**, supporting a constructive outlook for upcoming quarterly earnings.

**CLSA on Industrials**

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

**Macquarie on Industrials**

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

Jefferies on Industrials

Media reports highlight that government has approved four Chinese companies (TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric, New Northeast Electric, Taikai Electric) to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities.

Import restrictions still remain.

Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains.

Remain positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy and see the correction as a buying opportunity

**Stock Fundamental in detail                            
****@Fundamental3**

**Nomura on Industrials**

Overreaction to narrow exemption

Order explicitly states it creates no precedent

Market players read it as a competitive threat & sold off listed power-equipment stocks such as - GE Vernova T&D India (Buy), CG Power (Buy), Hitachi Energy Not rated) & Siemens Energy Not rated)

Exempted players won 9% of transmission-linked PGCIL tenders over FY09-20

Uneven capacity readiness across four entities: only one appears to be running near-optimal utilization

Exemption is explicitly sunset-bound, & grid infrastructure sits squarely in national-security bucket

Two-year window is too short to underwrite meaningful capacity expansion in India

**POWER T&D | Brokerage Views After Friday's Correction**

**Key Highlights**
- Brokerages divided on correction
- Chinese exemption valid for 2 years
- Demand outlook remains strong
- Margins remain key monitorable

**Brokerage Views**
- **Nomura:** Calls the sell-off an overreaction; exemption is narrow, temporary and does not set a precedent.
- **Jefferies:** Sees the correction as a buying opportunity; remains positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy.
- **Macquarie:** Exemption addresses current equipment shortages but could pressure margins if extended beyond two years.
- **CLSA:** Expects higher competition to weigh on pricing power and margins; advises caution on rich valuations.

**Key Takeaway**
- While **Nomura, Jefferies and Macquarie** believe the **two-year exemption for select Chinese manufacturers is unlikely to derail India's long-term power transmission capex cycle**, **CLSA** cautions that **higher competition could compress margins for domestic T&D equipment companies**, making **execution and valuations key factors to watch**.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__
